summary.intent: Migrate the gossamer app from React Native/Expo to Electron with file-based routing, add a CheckpointTimeline screen, and fix live WebSocket updates for the checkpoints UI. Also refactor the sessions view to use V2 checkpoint tables (CheckpointSessionMetadata) with real branch and intent data.
summary.outcome: Successfully migrated to Electron + Vite + React Router DOM, added CheckpointTimeline screen, fixed WebSocket broadcasts to watch CheckpointMetadata table, and wired up branch and intent fields from V2 checkpoint tables into the sessions table UI.

repo: expo-router defaults to an `app/` directory for routes, which collides with an Expo project already rooted at `app/`; fixed by setting `["expo-router", { "root": "routes" }]` in app.json
electron-vite outputs to `out/main/`, `out/preload/`, `out/renderer/` by default — not `dist-electron/`
The Session model has no `branch` field; branch is only tracked per-checkpoint in CheckpointSessionMetadata, joined by sessionId
The server's 2s WebSocket broadcast poller only watched the Event table, not CheckpointMetadata, so checkpoint updates were never broadcast
CheckpointSessionMetadata holds branch; CheckpointSessionSummary (a relation on CheckpointSessionMetadata) holds intent — must be included via `V2_SESSION_INCLUDE` or explicit Prisma include
The git worktree at `/tmp/gossamer-checkpoints` is cleared between macOS restarts, causing a non-fatal warning on server startup; can be fixed by pruning stale worktrees at startup
runIndex() only fired on 30s interval, not at startup — meaning checkpoints appeared with a delay after server start

code.finding: WebSocket broadcast poller — originally only tracked max Event.id; needed to also track max CheckpointMetadata.id to broadcast on new checkpoints
RN→Web shim: View→div with flex-direction:column, Text→div, TouchableOpacity→div[role=button], StyleSheet.create is a passthrough — allows most screen code to migrate with only import path changes
Uses MemoryRouter (not BrowserRouter) since Electron serves via file:// and has no real URL bar
Title computed from useLocation().pathname + state.title passed via navigate(path, { state: { title } }) — replaces Stack.Screen options per screen

workflow: Exit code 144 from background tasks means the process was killed by pkill during a restart — not a real failure, just stale task notifications
electron-vite dev mode sets ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL env var; main process uses this to loadURL in dev and loadFile in prod
When migrating RN MarkdownView to web, nested Text→div causes block-inside-inline issues; solution is to use a separate Inline renderer with span elements

friction: Multiple stale task-failed notifications from pkill'd background processes caused confusion about server health
expo toolchain auto-stripped manually added `include` array from tsconfig.json
package.json main field pointed to wrong electron-vite output path (`dist-electron/main.js` vs `out/main/index.js`), requiring a fix after first failed dev launch
Context window ran out mid-investigation of the WebSocket issue, requiring summary-based continuation

The CheckpointSessionSummary intent field required multiple layered edits to server.ts (mapSession, include clauses, both list and single endpoints) with several back-and-forth Edit calls to get right
open_items: Branch field on Session model: currently joined from CheckpointSessionMetadata but not stored natively on Session — could add a branch column to Session schema populated from SessionStart hook events
Git worktree prune on startup: fix was written but only takes effect after next rebuild/restart
CheckpointTimeline branch colors use a hash function for consistency — no persistent color assignment across sessions
SessionDetail and SessionTree screens were ported but not audited for correctness after Electron migration
The old app/src/screens/ and app/src/components/ directories were deleted — no git history preservation confirmed
Mantine table was referenced in user request for sessions view but actual implementation used custom HTML table with primitives shim — Mantine was not actually installed or used
